All tenant codes returned by `getUserCon: All tenant codes returned by `getUserContext()` are normalized to lowercase. This means `TenantA`, `TENANTA`, and `tenanta` are all treated as `tenanta`. When defining tenant codes in Cognito custom claims or HTTP headers, the case doesn't matter - internally they are always lowercase for consistent matching.

The `@mbc-cqrs-serverless/master` and `@: The `@mbc-cqrs-serverless/master` and `@mbc-cqrs-serverless/tenant` packages use `SettingTypeEnum.TENANT_COMMON = 'common'` (lowercase), consistent with `getUserContext()` normalization.
When using built-in methods (e.g., `crea: When using built-in methods (e.g., `createCommonTenantSetting`, `createCommonTenant`), data is stored under the `'common'` tenant code, which can be queried using the normalized lowercase tenant code.
